messages.content: Write a CBBC lineup from 2007, 2008, 2012 and 2021

2. Blue Peter

5. Horrible Histories

Write a convo about more forgotten shows (e.g Skunk Fu, DNN: Definitely Not Newsround, etc)

Person 1: "Hey, do you remember that show Skunk Fu? It was so random but I loved it!"

Person 2: "Oh yeah, with that kung fu fighting skunk and the colorful animation, that show was a classic! And what about DNN: Definitely Not Newsround? It was such a funny take on news reporting for kids."

Person 1: "Haha, yes! I used to crack up at the silly segments and humor in DNN. Remember Trapped? That show where kids had to solve puzzles to escape rooms? It was so intense!"

Person 2: "Oh my gosh, Trapped was so good! I always wanted to be on that show and test my puzzle-solving skills. And what about Hider in the House? The one where kids had to hide a celebrity in their house without their parents finding out?"

Person 1: "Yes, I loved Hider in the House! It was like a mix of hide-and-seek and a prank show, so entertaining. There were so many creative and fun shows back then that seem to have been forgotten now."
Write a letter asking if SF will ever return

Dear SF Productions,

I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to inquire about the potential return of one of my favorite shows, Skunk Fu. As a fan of the show, I have fond memories of watching the adventures of the kung fu fighting skunk and his friends.

I am wondering if there are any plans to bring back Skunk Fu for a new generation of viewers. The show had such a unique and entertaining concept, and I believe it would be well-received by audiences today.

If there are any updates or information regarding the possible revival of Skunk Fu, I would greatly appreciate it if you could share them with me. I would love to see the lovable characters and exciting storylines of the show once again.

Thank you for taking the time to consider my request. I look forward to hearing from you soon regarding the future of Skunk Fu.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
Write a reply from the Beeb saying it won't return (yet), but here are 6 other shows (STS and DM are the first 2)

Dear [Your Name],

Thank you for reaching out to us about the potential return of Skunk Fu. While we appreciate your enthusiasm for the show, we regret to inform you that there are currently no plans for its revival.

However, we understand your love for engaging and entertaining shows, and we would like to recommend six other series that you might enjoy. The first two recommendations are:

1. "Shaun the Sheep": Join Shaun and his mischievous flock in their hilarious adventures on the farm.
2. "Danger Mouse": Follow the daring exploits of the world's greatest secret agent, Danger Mouse, and his trusty sidekick Penfold.

Additionally, here are four more shows that you may find entertaining:

3. "The Worst Witch": Dive into the magical world of Mildred Hubble as she attends Miss Cackle's Academy for Witches.
4. "Horrible Histories": Discover history like never before with this entertaining and educational series.
5. "M.I. High": Join a group of young spies as they embark on thrilling missions and adventures.
6. "The Next Step": Follow the journey of a competitive dance troupe as they strive for excellence and success.

We hope that you find these recommendations enjoyable and that they provide you with hours of entertainment. Thank you for your continued support of CBBC and our programming.

Sincerely,
CBBC Programming Team
Write a reply from the kid saying they hate Next Step but LOVE Shaun, and ask if they could add TMNT

Dear CBBC Programming Team,

Thank you for your response and for recommending some shows to me. I absolutely love "Shaun the Sheep" - it's one of my all-time favorites! However, I must admit that "The Next Step" isn't really my cup of tea. I prefer shows with more adventure and excitement.

I was wondering if you could consider adding "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles" (TMNT) to your lineup. I think this action-packed series with Leonardo, Michelangelo, Donatello, and Raphael fighting crime in the sewers of New York City would be a fantastic addition to your schedule. The turtles have always been my heroes, and I would love to see their adventures on CBBC.

Thank you for taking the time to read my message and for considering my suggestion. I hope to see some new and exciting shows included in the lineup soon.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
Write a follow up where he still really misses it

Dear CBBC Programming Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to circle back to my previous request about adding "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles" (TMNT) to your lineup. Despite your recommendations, I still find myself really missing the action and excitement of that show. The adventures of the crime-fighting turtles and their battles against Shredder and his minions always kept me on the edge of my seat.

I understand that programming decisions can be complex, but I wanted to emphasize how much I would love to see TMNT back on CBBC. The dynamic characters, thrilling storylines, and lessons in teamwork and bravery that the show offered are truly missed.

I appreciate your consideration and thank you for your dedication to providing quality content for viewers like me. I hope to hear some positive news regarding the inclusion of TMNT in the future.

Thank you for your time and understanding.

Warm regards,
[Your Name]
Write a convo where the kid asks for Skunk back

Kid: "Hey, have you heard anything about Skunk Fu coming back to CBBC? I really miss that show."

Friend: "Oh yeah, Skunk Fu was awesome! I remember watching it all the time. I haven't heard anything about it returning, but I hope they bring it back. The kung fu skunk was so cool!"

Kid: "I know, right? I've been watching some other shows, but nothing quite compares to the adventures of Skunk and his friends. I wish they would bring it back for a new generation to enjoy."

Friend: "Maybe we can write to CBBC and ask them to bring Skunk Fu back. It's such a unique and entertaining show, I'm sure there are others who miss it too."

Kid: "That's a great idea! I'll send them a letter and let them know how much I want to see Skunk Fu back on TV. Who knows, maybe they'll consider it if they see how many fans are eager for its return."

Friend: "Let's do it! Skunk Fu deserves a comeback, and we can make our voices heard. Fingers crossed that CBBC listens to our requests."

Kid: "Absolutely! Skunk Fu fans unite!"

Friend: "Skunk Fu forever!"
Write a letter back from the Beeb admitting that TNS is more of a BBC 3 show and that they're junking it

Dear [Your Name],

Thank you for your passionate letter expressing your love for Skunk Fu and your desire to see it return to CBBC. We appreciate your dedication to the show and understand how much it meant to you.

However, we regret to inform you that after careful consideration and a review of our programming lineup, we have come to the decision to discontinue "The Next Step" on CBBC. We recognize that the show may not align with the overall tone and target audience of the channel, and therefore, we have decided to remove it from our schedule.

Please know that this decision was not made lightly, and we understand that it may disappoint some viewers. We value your feedback and interest in our programming, and we will take your thoughts into account as we continue to shape the content on CBBC.

We hope that you understand our decision and that you continue to enjoy the other shows and content on the channel. Thank you for your support and for being a vital part of the CBBC community.

Sincerely,
CBBC Programming Team
Write a reply where he's glad Step is gone, and ask what's taking it's place, to his suprise 2 shows are taking its spaces, both of which he asked for

Dear CBBC Programming Team,

Thank you for your response regarding the discontinuation of "The Next Step" on CBBC. I completely understand the decision and actually feel relieved as it never quite matched my interests. It's great to see that you are considering the audience's preferences when curating the programming lineup.

I'm curious to know what will be taking the place of "The Next Step" on CBBC. It would be interesting to see what new shows will be featured on the channel and how they will captivate the audience. 

To my surprise, I was delighted to discover that you have decided to include not just one but two shows that I had suggested in my previous messages - "Shaun the Sheep" and "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles." I couldn't be happier to see these fantastic shows making their way onto CBBC.

Thank you for listening to viewer feedback and for always striving to provide entertaining and engaging content for your audience. I am looking forward to enjoying these new additions to the CBBC lineup.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
Write a reply asking if Skunk is returning as well and also DNN

Dear CBBC Programming Team,

Thank you for the information regarding the new additions to the CBBC lineup, including "Shaun the Sheep" and "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles." I am thrilled to see these shows making their way onto the channel and can't wait to watch them.

I was wondering if there are any plans to bring back "Skunk Fu" as well. As I mentioned before, "Skunk Fu" was one of my favorite shows, and I would love to see it return for a new generation of viewers to enjoy. The adventures of the kung fu-skilled skunk and his friends were always a delight to watch.

Additionally, I wanted to inquire about the possibility of "DNN: Definitely Not Newsround" making a return. This show had a unique and humorous take on news reporting for kids, and it would be fantastic to see it back on CBBC.

Thank you for considering my inquiries, and I appreciate your dedication to providing quality programming for viewers like me. I look forward to hearing any updates regarding the potential return of "Skunk Fu" and "DNN."

Best regards,
[Your Name]
